source_title: 8348d2be feat(move-5c): end-to-end demo fixture — lower-third over footage + audio
subject: active demo timeline lineage
claim: The currently active corpus/demo/demo.mlt was introduced as an explicitly audio-bearing fixture and has not changed since this commit; its A1 source is corpus/tone.wav at about -6 dB with fades.
evidence_quote: Assemble the committed Move-5 demo ... and a -6dB tone audio bed. ... The exported mp4 has exactly one video + one audio stream.
impact: Recent viewer/compositor commits did not rewrite the active timeline, so a newly observed silence on this route is more likely in preview resolution, decode, graph, or output routing than in document history.
implication: Use corpus/tone.wav/A1 as a stable regression probe and compare preview behavior against the independently proven export audio.
notes: git log --follow for corpus/demo/demo.mlt ends at 8348d2be; current XML still names corpus/tone.wav and gain 0.5011872336272722.

source_title: 0ec3f53 feat(viewer): live footage preview — Tier 0 source-frame seeking
subject: original editor audio ownership
claim: Tier 0 made the visible pooled HTMLVideoElement itself the audio source and invoked native play/pause during clock playback.
evidence_quote: During playback the element plays natively for audio + motion; the RAF master clock still owns the integer frame.
impact: The original preview delegated container decode and audible output to the browser media element, with autoplay errors caught only around element.play().
implication: Later silence should be compared across the June 30 ownership switch: current preview no longer exercises this native media-element audio path.

source_title: a71c5ca feat(viewer): Tier 2b live preview — Web Audio graph slaved to the clock
subject: Web Audio mixer replacement
claim: Tier 2b replaced native media-element audio with a Web Audio mixer that fetches each entire source, calls decodeAudioData, and intentionally turns every non-OK response or decode exception into a null buffer that is silently skipped.
evidence_quote: Returns null on a fetch/decode failure (a missing audio track, an unsupported container) — that clip is silently skipped. ... if (!res.ok) return null; ... } catch { return null; }
impact: The editor can advance normally with context/clock state looking healthy while producing no scheduled audible source, and the causal fetch/decode error is erased.
implication: This is the highest-value regression seam: inspect actual media fetch/decode outcomes, not merely AudioContext running or clock movement.

source_title: a71c5ca feat(viewer): Tier 2b live preview — Web Audio graph slaved to the clock
subject: initial audio resolver scope
claim: The first Web Audio resolver scheduled only timeline.tracks.audio and therefore omitted embedded audio on ordinary video-track footage.
evidence_quote: for (const track of timeline.tracks.audio) { trackIds.push(track.id); placeAudioTrack(track, clips); } return { clips, trackIds };
impact: The Web Audio migration itself introduced a complete-silence failure mode for common A/V clips even though exports remained audible.
implication: History establishes that schedule eligibility omissions—not OS output alone—have previously caused this exact symptom.

source_title: 4735c6a fix(viewer): preview sources embedded audio from video-track clips
subject: known editor-silence repair
claim: A same-day fix explicitly diagnosed that normal MP4 clips on V1 scheduled zero audio and expanded resolveAudio to include video-track embedded audio.
evidence_quote: The live-preview audio graph only scheduled clips from tracks.audio, so a normal MP4 on a video track ... played NO audio in preview ... scheduledClips was 0, now 2.
impact: This is a directly analogous historical incident: render audio existed, the editor moved, but resolver output was empty.
implication: Any current zero-schedule observation should be treated as a concrete regression in eligibility/data shape, not dismissed as generic desktop audio behavior.

source_title: 409d22a feat(ir,viewer): Remotion overlays are first-class movable clips
subject: intentional overlay silence
claim: Composition-bearing baked Remotion clips were intentionally excluded from video-track audio scheduling because their audio was assumed silent/incidental.
evidence_quote: a Remotion overlay is ... skipped for audio ... Stops the wasteful fetch of the overlay .mov for its silent audio.
impact: If a genuinely sound-bearing clip is mislabeled with composition metadata, it will be deterministically silent in preview by design even though its file contains audio.
implication: Inspect composition/graphic classification on the active audible producer before blaming decode or device output.

subject: StrictMode graph recreation
claim: A later lifecycle fix replaced a revision-only guard with an AudioScheduleGate keyed by graph identity plus revision and releases the graph on cleanup, specifically so the recreated live StrictMode graph is scheduled.
evidence_quote: it("schedules a recreated StrictMode audio graph at the same revision") ... gate.release(firstGraph); expect(gate.shouldSchedule(recreatedGraph, 7)).toBe(true);
impact: The previously plausible StrictMode second-mount silence was intentionally addressed, and current blame shows the fix remains in place.
implication: Do not prioritize stale revision gating unless runtime evidence contradicts the gate contract; investigate downstream decode/output first.

source_title: 19b8118 fix(viewer): honor detached audio and normalize waveforms
subject: detached A/V eligibility change
claim: The newest audio resolver change suppresses only producers whose audio selectors explicitly disable decode, so a detached pair schedules the audio-only half while preventing the video-only half from double-playing the same source.
evidence_quote: if (streams.astream != null) return streams.astream !== -1; if (streams.audioIndex != null) return streams.audioIndex !== -1; ... expect(clips).toHaveLength(1); expect(clips[0]?.uuid).toBe("a");
impact: This commit can reduce scheduled clip count for detached media, but its tested intended result is one audible source—not total silence.
implication: It is a candidate only if the active audio half was parsed with an unexpected -1 audio selector; the stable demo A1 has no such selectors, so this change cannot explain demo silence directly.

source_title: 19b8118 fix(viewer): honor detached audio and normalize waveforms
subject: waveform normalization semantics
claim: The waveform half of the commit applies display-only gain to SVG amplitudes and explicitly does not alter playback gain.
evidence_quote: This never changes playback gain; it only changes the SVG envelope.
impact: A large visible waveform proves that peak extraction found nonzero samples, but it does not prove the AudioGraph fetched, decoded, scheduled, routed, or emitted those samples.
implication: Do not use waveform visibility as evidence against a preview audio-path failure.

source_title: 802b821 Add subject compositing and animated layout controls
subject: recent subject-compositor change
claim: The recent subject-compositor commit did not modify AudioGraph or resolveAudio; it adds the cutout as a plain video clip via buildClip with no composition identity or stream-disable selectors.
evidence_quote: const cutout = buildClip(args.cutoutResource, { id: cutoutClipId, in: inFrame, out: inFrame + args.durationFrames - 1, ... });
impact: It is not a direct mixer regression. By inference, the generic resolver may attempt to decode audio from a silent alpha cutout, but failure is silently skipped and does not mute the underlying target/audio track.
implication: Keep this commit below 19b8118 and a71c5ca in causal priority unless runtime scheduling shows cutout classification displaced the intended source.
notes: Commit stat touches src/actions/compositing.ts and visual resolve/compositor geometry, but no viewer/src/audio or viewer/src/resolveAudio path.

source_title: e9e4709 Preserve cumulative alpha composite topology
subject: recent cumulative-composite fix
claim: The follow-up commit changes only qtblend aTrack preservation when reusing a visual transition; it contains no audio resolver, graph, mute, volume, or routing change.
evidence_quote: Reusing an established qtblend must preserve its compositing root ... aTrack = baseTransition.aTrack;
impact: Temporal proximity to the report is misleading; this commit cannot directly silence editor audio.
implication: Rule it out absent evidence that a separate timeline mutation altered the actual audio producer.

source_title: a088949 feat(harness): prove media in actual WKWebView
subject: native WK audio assurance boundary
claim: The actual-WKWebView audio proof fetches bytes, decodeAudioData's them, computes RMS, and closes the AudioContext; it never creates a source node, connects to destination, resumes for output, or measures emitted sound.
evidence_quote: const buffer = await audioContext.decodeAudioData(await response.arrayBuffer()); ... return { rms, channels: buffer.numberOfChannels, sample_rate: buffer.sampleRate }; ... await audioContext.close();
impact: The native gate can pass while the real editor remains silent due to scheduling, master gain, context resume, sink, or destination-output failures.
implication: A passing H07 WK audio cell rules in decoder support only; it is not audible-playback evidence.

source_title: 074d262 fix(harness): drive H07 through product media paths
subject: product playback assertion boundary
claim: The product transport/audio lifecycle waits only for the AudioGraph's internal playing boolean before pausing again; it does not require contextState running, a buffered resource, a scheduled clip, or a nonzero output signal.
evidence_quote: await page.keyboard.press("Space"); await page.waitForFunction(() => ... __veanMediaState?.().audio?.playing === true); await page.waitForTimeout(200); await page.keyboard.press("Space");
impact: The primary product harness can certify the play transition even when every loadBuffer call returns null and nothing reaches speakers.
implication: The historical verification story has a precise blind spot that is consistent with the reported symptom surviving all existing gates.
